title: Book Trilogy Atlas
public_claim: Book Trilogy Atlas is a manuscript-control atlas for a governed three-book inquiry into power, democratic formation, violence, memory, and repair.
distinctive_mechanism: It maps seventy-two chapters through canonical IDs, ReaderEdition targets, FinalProof argument backbone, ConstructionKits, PassageTargets, Democratic Dialectic voice material, bridge logic, dependency logic, terminology controls, chapter shape, chapter arcs, title state, visual identity, and rewrite governance across Public Book I, Public Book II, and Public Book III.
use_value: It matters because the trilogy's distinctive claim is architectural: power, democratic speech, and political repair have to be managed as one book system without flattening the three books into one voice or confusing public book order with canonical chapter IDs.

flagship_proof_builds.name: 72-chapter atlas proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: The atlas should show full chapter architecture, public/canonical mapping, title state, visual specificity, and source-governed rewrite workflow.
flagship_proof_builds.input: 72 chapter IDs, book order, chapter title ledger, visual state, and rewrite-status data
flagship_proof_builds.operation: bind each chapter to its book, argument role, rewrite target, visual/title state, and next dossier step
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: chapter-atlas proof record and next rewrite queue
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: select one chapter from each book and show canonical ID, public book, argument role, visual/title status, and next rewrite task

component_proofs.claim_supported: keeps the three-book system stable while public titles and presentation language are improved
component_proofs.input: 72 canonical chapter IDs, public book order, book-title logic, and chapter-title state
component_proofs.expected_output: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
component_proofs.verification_check: Apply Canonical Chapter Identity to one chapter dossier; confirm title, visual, source state, rewrite target, and handoff decision.

component_proofs.claim_supported: identifies what is readable but flattened, generic, or not yet book-appropriate in voice

minimum_public_demo: select one chapter from each book and show canonical ID, public book, argument role, visual/title status, and next rewrite task
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
